specification.topic-statement: The provider error categorization system maps HTTP status codes and provider-specific error fields to standardized error categories, and classifies categories by retry eligibility.

scope.in-scope: HTTP status code to error category mapping for each of the three providers (Anthropic, Google, OpenAI), provider-specific error code refinement (OpenAI only), and the retry eligibility classification shared across all providers.
scope.boundary: How error categories are used to decide whether to retry or abort a request is covered by the LLM response handling specification.
In-stream error events use a subset of these categories. Stream error events are documented in the individual provider streaming specifications.

field.name: NOT_FOUND
field.note: requested resource or model not found

field.name: UNKNOWN
field.note: unrecognized status code or error type

behavior.anthropic-http-mapping:
Anthropic HTTP status codes are mapped as follows: - 401, 403 → AUTH - 429 → RATE_LIMIT - 400 → INVALID_ARG - 404 → NOT_FOUND - 500, 529 → SERVER (529 is Anthropic's "overloaded" status) - All other codes → UNKNOWN
The error response body may contain a JSON error type and message, but the category is determined solely by the HTTP status code; the error body fields are not used for categorization.
behavior.google-http-mapping:
Google HTTP status codes are mapped as follows: - 400 → INVALID_ARG - 401, 403 → AUTH - 404 → NOT_FOUND - 429 → RATE_LIMIT - 500, 502, 503 → SERVER - 504 → TIMEOUT - All other codes → UNKNOWN
An error message is extracted from the JSON body's nested error.message field and formatted as "HTTP {code}: {message}". If the body cannot be parsed, the message falls back to "HTTP {code}".
behavior.openai-http-mapping:
OpenAI HTTP status codes are initially mapped as follows: - 401, 403 → AUTH - 429 → RATE_LIMIT - 400 → INVALID_ARG - 404 → NOT_FOUND - 500, 502, 503 → SERVER - All other codes → UNKNOWN
This initial category is then refined by inspecting the error JSON body:
1. If the error.type or error.code field contains the substring "content_filter", the category is overridden to CONTENT_FILTER regardless of the HTTP status.
2. If content filter was not detected, the error.code field may further refine the category: - "invalid_api_key" or "invalid_org" → AUTH - "rate_limit_exceeded" or "quota_exceeded" → RATE_LIMIT - "model_not_found" → NOT_FOUND
Code-based refinement takes precedence over HTTP-status-based categorization when the code is recognized.
behavior.retry-eligibility:
Regardless of provider, error categories are classified for retry eligibility as follows:
Retryable categories: - RATE_LIMIT - SERVER - TIMEOUT - NETWORK
Non-retryable categories: - AUTH - INVALID_ARG - NOT_FOUND - CONTENT_FILTER - UNKNOWN
This classification is queried by the request retry logic to determine whether to attempt the request again after a failure.
